subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Speaking Up for Safety
description: A micro-lesson that makes Speaking Up for Safety usable.

hook.text: A junior engineer saw O-ring data warning of failure before the Challenger launch. He was overruled.

buildup.text: Psychological safety—the ability to speak up without punishment—is critical for physical safety.

discovery.text: Workers who feel safe to report hazards catch problems before they become disasters.

twist.text: Hierarchies suppress safety communication—junior workers defer to seniors even when they're wrong.

climax.text: Aviation's Crew Resource Management gives every crew member authority to challenge unsafe decisions.

punchline.text: The quietest voice in the room might have the most important safety warning.

quiz.question: What does psychological safety enable in the workplace?
options: Workers reporting hazards without fear of punishment
